note: Always-in-context spine for discodisco. Facts must be defensible — sourced from research/, dated, and quotable in a sales conversation. Update with care.

cards.title: Vercel platform — what it actually is, in 2026
cards.one_liner: The frontend cloud and AI app platform: Edge Network + Fluid Compute + AI Gateway/SDK + v0, with Next.js as the gravitational center.
facts: Fluid Compute powers 45B+ weekly requests across the platform.
Series F (Oct 2025): $300M raised, ~$9.3B valuation, 82% YoY top-line growth, doubled user base.
Next.js exceeded 500M downloads in 12 months; remains the dominant React meta-framework.
Core surface: Edge Network, Functions (Fluid), Cache Components / ISR, Routing Middleware (formerly middleware), AI Gateway, AI SDK, Workflow, Sandbox, v0.
Self-Driving Infrastructure thesis: platform handles compute model selection, scaling, caching, and now AI provider routing — so app teams don't operate infra.
cards.use_when: Always. Anchors the model in what Vercel is before discussing specific products.

cards.title: Fluid Compute + Active CPU pricing — the cost story
cards.one_liner: Fluid pauses billing during I/O wait. Active CPU pricing is ~53% cheaper baseline and up to 90% cheaper for AI/I/O-heavy workloads — directly fixes the 'serverless is expensive for AI' objection.
facts: Active CPU pricing (GA June 2025): $0.128/CPU-hour vs. legacy ~$0.318/CPU-hour — 53% cheaper.
Up to 90% savings for AI inference workloads where most time is idle waiting on LLM responses.
Typical 5s LLM call → ~50ms active CPU = ~99% reduction in billable CPU time vs. duration-based billing.
300s default function timeout (vs. 60–90s legacy serverless); Fluid extends further on appropriate plans.
In-function concurrency: a single Fluid instance handles many concurrent I/O-bound requests instead of spinning new cold instances.
AI Gateway sits on the same CDN that powers trillions of requests/year — sub-20ms gateway overhead.
cards.use_when: Cost objections, AI workload deals, comparisons vs. AWS Lambda / Netlify Functions / Cloudflare Workers pricing.

cards.title: Vercel's AI stack — Gateway, SDK, v0, Sandbox, Workflow
cards.one_liner: Unified AI development surface: one API key (Gateway) for 20+ providers, AI SDK 6 with ToolLoopAgent + MCP, v0 for prompt-to-production UI, Sandbox for safe code exec.
facts: AI Gateway: 20+ LLM providers behind one API, zero token markup (pay provider list prices), built-in failover, observability.
AI SDK 6: TypeScript-first; ToolLoopAgent class is the production-ready agent primitive; native MCP (Model Context Protocol) with HTTP transport + OAuth.
v0: prompt-to-app product; v0-1.5-lg has 512K-token context; reportedly $42M ARR (~21% of Vercel revenue).
Sandbox: Firecracker microVMs with credential brokering — inject API keys into outgoing requests without exposing them to executed code. Notion Workers runs millions of concurrent Sandbox executions.
Workflow (WDK): durable, crash-safe, step-based orchestration for long-running AI/agent tasks with pause/resume + retries.
cards.use_when: AI/agent product teams; prospects building chatbots, agents, RAG, or anything LLM-shaped.

cards.title: ICP — three buyer profiles where Vercel actually fits
cards.one_liner: Next.js-native growth startups, enterprise digital commerce, and AI-native product teams. Outside these, qualify hard.
facts: **Next.js-native growth startups (Series A–C, ~10–100 eng):** Already on Next.js or about to be. Pain: own infra is slowing shipping; AWS bill creeping. Decision driver: DX + speed-to-ship. Champion is usually a senior IC or eng lead.

**Enterprise digital commerce / B2C (retail, media, travel):** Black Friday / peak-traffic problems, A/B testing velocity, conversion lift. Champion: dir/VP of digital + a frontend platform team. Decision driver: ISR + edge perf + preview envs.

**AI-native product teams:** Building chatbots/agents/copilots, hitting Lambda cost ceilings on idle waits. Decision driver: Fluid Active CPU + Gateway unification. Champion: founding eng or AI platform lead.

Anti-ICP signals: WebSocket-heavy realtime (no native support), heavy background-job workloads (need Inngest/QStash/Trigger.dev), strict on-prem / air-gap / FedRAMP-required, frameworks-other-than-Next where Netlify or Cloudflare may serve as well.
cards.use_when: Always — gates whether Vercel is even the right call before going deeper.

cards.title: Top case studies — pick the closest match by industry/buyer
cards.one_liner: Forrester ROI for the CFO; sportswear/Helly Hansen for retail; Stripe/Notion/Avalara for the AI builder.
facts: **Forrester TEI (composite):** 264% ROI over 3 years, $9.53M NPV; 90% less time managing frontend infra; 80% less time deploying; 4x more major site enhancements/year.
**Global sportswear brand (NYSE, ~$6B rev):** $10M incremental quarterly online sales; 5x conversion lift at peak; 125k visitors/hour at peak; first Black Friday with zero downtime.

**Helly Hansen:** 80% YoY Black Friday growth with zero downtime; 2x conversion lift; marketing spend tripled mid-campaign because it was so profitable; A/B test cadence went from 1/month → 5–6/sprint.
**Stripe (v0):** built by a non-engineer on one flight; 288% WAU growth (23 → 92); ~10 new internal presentations/week; 30–40 FTE hours saved/week.
**Avalara (v0):** two patent-pending products in ~60 days; concept-to-prototype in days vs. months.
**Notion Workers (Sandbox):** millions of concurrent executions of arbitrary third-party code, secured via Firecracker + credential injection.
**Cruise Critic, Stably, Gamma, Grep (CRA→Next.js):** mid-market migration / perf / build-time wins; available as long-tail RAG.
cards.use_when: Drop a single closest-match case study (AE) or top 3 with rationale (SE/SA).

cards.title: Real technical pushbacks — be ready
cards.one_liner: WebSockets, background jobs, FedRAMP, EU data residency, and one famous CVE. Don't pretend these don't exist.
facts: **No native WebSocket support** — long-running stateful connections aren't first-class. Workaround: pair with Pusher/Ably/Convex.
**No background-jobs primitive** — push to Inngest, QStash, or Trigger.dev. Workflow (WDK) is durable but request/event-shaped, not a queue replacement.
**Function timeout** — 60s on Pro by default; Fluid extends meaningfully on Enterprise; not a fit for hour-long jobs.
**CVE-2025-29927 (CVSS 9.1):** Next.js Middleware authorization bypass via `x-middleware-subrequest` header — affected 11–15. Patched. SAs should know it exists; expect security teams to ask.
**Data residency:** No data is stored permanently inside EU regions (compute can run there; persistence is the gap). Sovereign-cloud / FedRAMP customers are out of fit.
**ISR / edge-config write costs** ($4.00/1M ISR writes, $5.00/500 edge-config writes) compound on aggressive revalidation — catch this in scoping.
cards.use_when: Objection-prep section. Use only when the prospect's signals match (don't volunteer FedRAMP for a 20-person AI startup).

cards.title: Real commercial pushbacks — pricing, lock-in, support
cards.one_liner: Pricing unpredictability is the #1 objection. Surface it first, then frame Active CPU + Spend Management as the answer.
facts: **Pricing unpredictability** is the most-cited objection — multi-dimensional metering (compute + bandwidth + ISR + edge config + image optimization) makes monthly forecasting hard.
**Public horror stories:** Riley Walz $46,485 bill (Feb 2026, static site, bandwidth overage); Cara $98,280 in Functions charges during a viral surge.
**No hard spending caps** historically — Spend Management (default $200 alert on new Pro teams, hard-pause optional) is the answer; lead with this in objection prep.

**Vendor lock-in at the runtime layer:** ISR, Routing Middleware, image optimization, on-demand `revalidatePath()` are Vercel-native. Self-hosting Next.js loses or degrades these. Frame as 'platform value, not artificial lock-in.'
**Support tier reality:** Pro has no SLA; Enterprise gets response-time guarantees. For mid-market deals where support matters, this lives in the deal scope.
**Enterprise entry point:** ~$20K–$25K/year typical floor; v0 Enterprise has been seen at ~$100/user/month + ~$18K platform fee for 20 seats.
cards.use_when: Pricing/finance objections, procurement conversations, anyone burned by previous serverless bills.

cards.title: Competitive read — Netlify, Cloudflare, AWS Amplify
cards.one_liner: Netlify is the most common comparison and the gap is widening. Cloudflare wins on edge/cost stories. Amplify is enterprise-priced but operationally complex.
facts: **vs. Netlify:** Vercel ~$200M ARR @ ~80% YoY (May 2025) vs. Netlify ~$46M @ ~40% YoY. Vercel $9.3B valuation vs. Netlify $2B (no round since Nov 2021). Fluid + Active CPU pricing has no Netlify equivalent. On-demand ISR (revalidatePath) is Vercel-only; Netlify supports time-based ISR.

**vs. Netlify (where they win):** framework-agnostic story (Astro / SvelteKit / Nuxt treated equally), Netlify Connect for CMS-heavy data unification, free tier permits commercial use (Vercel doesn't), Bolt.new partnership traction.
**vs. Cloudflare Workers / Pages:** Cloudflare wins on raw edge cost and global compute footprint; Vercel wins on Next.js DX, framework ergonomics, ISR, and AI SDK integration.

**vs. AWS Amplify:** rarely beats Vercel on velocity; usually appears when a customer has heavy AWS commit / IAM-deep integration. Frame as 'Amplify is Lambda + S3 + CloudFront stitched by you; Vercel is the platform.'
**v0 has no real competitor at parity** for prompt-to-production-React-code with Vercel deploy in the loop — Netlify Create (acquired) is CMS-shaped, not code-gen.
cards.use_when: Competitive section (SE/SA). For AE: just call the likely competitor and 1–2 differentiators.

cards.title: MEDDPICC + SPICED dual-tag legend
cards.one_liner: Every discovery question must dual-tag against both methodologies so reps can show qualification work to managers using either framework.
facts: **MEDDPICC** = Metrics, Economic buyer, Decision criteria, Decision process, Paper process, Identify pain, Champion, Competition.
**SPICED** = Situation, Pain, Impact, Critical event, Decision.
Dual-tag rule: each discovery question must be tagged with at least one MEDDPICC letter AND at least one SPICED letter. If a question maps to neither, it's filler — cut it.
Coaching note (the WHY): for SE/SA mode, every question also gets a 1-line coaching note explaining what skilled-listening signals the rep should listen for in the answer.
cards.use_when: Always. Every generated discovery question must come back with both tags. The model uses this card as a compliance check.
